name: worktree-warp-mode
title: Worktree Warp Mode — Session Lookup, Header Contract, and Structured Downgrade
purpose: Capability: Defines when adv_worktree_create selects mode:warp, the already-warped block contract, the structured downgrade_reason discriminated union emitted on every warp→terminal fallback, the SDK-routed session lookup requirement, and the x-opencode-directory header contract on raw-fetch workspace endpoints.

requirements.title: Warp Mode Selection
requirements.body: adv_worktree_create selects mode:warp only when ALL of the following are true: (a) worktree config declares mode:warp; (b) the OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_WORKSPACES or OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL env var is exactly 'true'; (c) runtime context includes serverUrl, sessionID, and an SDK client; (d) session lookup via client.session.get succeeds with no existing workspaceID; (e) GET /experimental/workspace is reachable. If any condition fails, the tool MUST downgrade to mode:terminal (or return SESSION_ALREADY_WARPED if condition (d) reveals an existing workspaceID).

scenarios.title: All preconditions met → mode:warp
given: Config mode:warp, env flag enabled, serverUrl+sessionID+client present
Session lookup returns { ok: true, workspaceID: null }
then: Tool output contains mode:"warp" and a workspaceID

scenarios.title: Missing runtime context → downgrade
then: Tool output contains a downgrade_reason matching the missing field

requirements.title: Already-Warped Block Contract
requirements.body: When session lookup returns { ok: true, workspaceID: <non-null> }, adv_worktree_create MUST return error:"SESSION_ALREADY_WARPED" with the sessionID, workspaceID, and a hint to open a fresh OpenCode session. The tool MUST NOT downgrade to mode:terminal and MUST NOT emit a downgrade_reason. This is a block, not a fallback.

scenarios.title: Workspace-id present → block, not downgrade
given: Session lookup returns { ok: true, workspaceID: "ws-existing" }
then: Tool output does NOT contain downgrade_reason
Tool output does NOT contain mode:"terminal"

requirements.title: Downgrade Reason Discriminated Union
requirements.body: On every mode:warp → mode:terminal downgrade path, adv_worktree_create MUST include a structured downgrade_reason field in tool output with one of the following discriminated-union variants: { kind: "missing_server" } | { kind: "missing_session" } | { kind: "missing_client" } | { kind: "flag_disabled" } | { kind: "lookup_failed", status?: number, detail?: string } | { kind: "endpoint_unreachable" } | { kind: "warp_failed", detail: string, cleanupFailed?: boolean }. The legacy warning:string field MUST also be present for back-compat human readability. The downgrade_reason field is the structural channel agents branch on.

scenarios.title: Every fallback path emits downgrade_reason
then: downgrade_reason.kind matches the specific failure category
Tool output also contains warning string

scenarios.title: Post-create warp failure includes detail and optional cleanupFailed
given: Workspace was created but warpSession threw
then: downgrade_reason.detail contains the warp error message

requirements.title: SDK-Routed Session Lookup
requirements.body: Session lookup MUST route through the v1 SDK client's typed method: client.session.get({ path: { id: sessionID } }). Raw fetch to /session/:id is disallowed in plugin/src/utils/workspace-warp.ts and plugin/src/tools/worktree/index.ts because it bypasses the SDK's request interceptor which attaches x-opencode-directory automatically. The lookup function returns a structured SessionLookupResult tuple { ok: true, workspaceID: string | null } | { ok: false, status?: number, detail: string } and never throws.

scenarios.title: Lookup never throws
given: The SDK client.session.get rejects with a network error

requirements.title: x-opencode-directory Header on Workspace Endpoints
requirements.body: All raw-fetch calls to /experimental/workspace/* in plugin/src/utils/workspace-warp.ts MUST attach the x-opencode-directory header with value encodeURIComponent(deps.directory). This applies to: workspaceAndWarpAvailable (GET), createAdvWorkspace (POST), warpSession (POST), deleteAdvWorkspace (DELETE), findWorkspaceByDirectory (GET). The header drives OpenCode's Instance.project.id resolution so session storage is namespaced correctly.

scenarios.title: Header attached on every workspace endpoint
given: Any of the 5 workspace-warp.ts functions is called
scenarios.when: The raw fetch request is built
then: The request headers include x-opencode-directory: <encoded directory>

requirements.title: Client Threading From Plugin Input
requirements.body: The v1 SDK client from PluginInput.client MUST be threaded through advancePluginImpl → createToolMap → AdvWorktreeCreateRuntime to enable rq-warpModeContract04. createToolMap accepts an optional client parameter; adv_worktree_create / adv_worktree_delete / adv_worktree_cleanup forward the client into their runtime options. Legacy standalone aliases (worktree_create / worktree_delete / worktree_cleanup) MUST NOT be registered by the ADV tool registry. If client is absent at runtime, adv_worktree_create emits downgrade_reason { kind: "missing_client" } and falls back to mode:terminal — it does not crash.

scenarios.title: Client present → forwarded to runtime
then: The execute call receives runtime with client matching PluginInput.client

scenarios.title: Client absent → missing_client downgrade
given: Runtime context does not include a client
